title: Chapter 12: Feral
content: Day 12 began with Seraphina screaming.

Marcus was out of bed before his eyes fully opened, bare feet slapping tile, heart slamming against his ribs. He found Sera in the living room, on her knees, fingers digging into her scalp, grey eyes wide and filming over with something that wasn't her.
"Get *out*," she snarled—but the voice was wrong. Layered. Overlaid with something deeper, older, a frequency that vibrated in his teeth.
"Sera!" He dropped beside her. "Sera, look at me—"

Her head snapped toward him. Her pupils had shrunk to pinpoints. Her lip curled, and he saw her teeth—not human teeth anymore, something sharper, something that belonged in a mouth that had forgotten how to do anything except bite.

**[WARNING: ALPHA SIGNAL OVERRIDE DETECTED]**
**[Seraphina Vale is experiencing a forced reversion attempt]**
**[The Alpha is attempting to reassert control over her zombie consciousness]**
**[Current Resistance: 78% — but declining]**
**[If resistance falls below 30%, she will enter Feral state and attack any human in range]**
"Marcus—" Sera's voice cracked, split between her own voice and something else. "Run. *Run*—"

Her hand shot toward him—not to grab, to push. She shoved him backward with enough force to send him sliding across the floor, and then she was on her feet, swaying, fighting her own body like a puppet severing its strings.
Elena appeared in the hallway, first aid kit clutched to her chest, face pale. "What's happening?"
"The Alpha. It's trying to take her." Marcus scrambled up. "Sera—fight it!"

"I'm *trying*—" A sound ripped from her throat, half-scream half-growl, and she slammed her own head against the titanium wall hard enough to dent it. The impact echoed through the safehouse like a gong.

She hit herself again. And again. Fighting the thing inside her skull with the only weapon she had—pain, the physical kind, the kind that cut through the Alpha's signal like static through a weak radio station.
"Sera, stop!" Marcus grabbed her arm. Her skin was ice—colder than he'd ever felt, cold enough to burn. "Stop hurting yourself!"
"If I stop—" Her teeth clenched. "—it *wins*—"

"Then let me help." He grabbed her other arm. Pulled her away from the wall. She was stronger than him—infinitely stronger—but she wasn't fighting *him*. She was letting him move her, choosing to be moved, trusting him enough to surrender control of her body to his hands.
He pressed his forehead to hers. The same position as the wall-slam. The same proximity. The same intimacy that had forced her instincts into submission once before.
"Sera. Listen to my voice. Not the Alpha's. *Mine*."

The grey of her eyes roiled like a storm-tossed sea. Two forces warring behind those irises— the Alpha's ancient, overwhelming command, and the bond she'd forged with a shut-in gamer who'd offered her saltines on day one.
"Marcus..." His name came out shattered. "I can feel it. It's *inside* my head. It's showing me things. Hundreds of zombies. Moving together. Coming—" Her body convulsed. "—*coming here*."

**[ALPHA SIGNAL ANALYSIS]**
**[The Alpha has located Seraphina Vale through her resistance signature.]**
**[Every time she blocks its signal, she creates a "shadow" in its awareness — evidence of something that doesn't obey.]**
**[It is now mobilizing a horde toward Oakwood Heights.]**
**[Estimated time of arrival: 36-48 hours]**
**[Horde size: 200+ evolved zombies, coordinated by Alpha]**
Two hundred. Two hundred zombies, coordinated, evolving, led by something that could override Sera's will from three miles away.
"I won't—" Sera's hands fisted in his shirt. "I won't let it take me. If I go feral—if I attack you or Elena—you have to—"
"I'm not killing you."
"Marcus—"

"*No.*" He said it with a certainty he didn't know he possessed. "We don't do that. We don't give up on people. The System gave me a bond with you for a reason, and I'm not breaking it because some overgrown corpse with a god complex decided to knock on the wrong door."

Sera stared at him. The Alpha's signal was still there—he could feel her trembling, feel the cold radiating off her in waves—but something else was there too. The bond. The imprinting. The connection that had told her mind it was safe to come back from the void because someone was waiting.
Her breathing steadied. Not because she needed to breathe—but because the rhythm of his breathing had become her anchor, and her body synced to it automatically.

**[RESISTANCE RECOVERING: 78% → 85%]**
**[Bond reinforcement effective.]**
**[Physical proximity + emotional anchor + vocal command = 92% resistance success rate]**
**[Recommendation: Maintain close proximity until Alpha signal passes]**
He led her to the couch. Sat her down. Sat beside her, her hand in his, her shoulder against his, the cold of her body slowly receding as the Alpha's signal ebbed.
Elena sat in the armchair across from them, watching with wide eyes. "She okay?"
"For now." Marcus didn't let go of Sera's hand. "But the Alpha knows we're here. It's sending a horde."
"How big?"
"Two hundred. Give or take."

Elena's face went through something—fear, calculation, resolve—before landing on a peculiar calm. "Then we have thirty-six hours to prepare. First, we fortify. Second, we plan. Third, we survive." She stood. "I'll inventory the medical bay. If there's fighting, there'll be injuries."

She walked to the medical bay without looking back, and Marcus realized he'd underestimated her. Elena wasn't just a victim who'd been rescued. She was a survivor who'd been *waiting* for a fight worth fighting.

**[NEW QUEST AVAILABLE]**
**[QUEST: Against the Horde]**
**[The Alpha has dispatched a coordinated horde toward your location.]**
**[ETA: 36-48 hours]**
**[You must prepare your safehouse and party for siege.]**
**[Objectives:]**
**[- Fortify all entry points (0/6)]**
**[- Complete at least 1 combat-related milestone with Sera]**
**[- Ensure Elena's medical bay is fully supplied]**
**[Rewards: Perimeter Defense System, 1x Legendary Feed Item, Party Combat Synergy Bonus]**
**[Failure: Safehouse destruction, party death]**
**[Accept? Y/N]**
**[ACCEPTED — NO ALTERNATIVE]**
The System wasn't giving him a choice. The quest auto-accepted because there was no alternative—either prepare or die.

Marcus looked at Sera. Her eyes had cleared, the Alpha's fog pushed back behind the walls of their bond. She was still trembling, still cold, still holding his hand with grip strength that could crush concrete.
But she was *here*. Present. Fighting.
"We have thirty-six hours," he said. "What do you need?"
Sera thought. The CEO was back—strategy mode engaged, fear compartmentalized, options calculated.

"I need to feed," she said. "My resistance scales with my bond strength, and my bond strength scales with my evolution. If I can reach Stage 4, I can project a counter-frequency that disrupts the Alpha's coordination. It won't stop the horde, but it'll make them disorganized. Disorganized zombies are manageable. Coordinated zombies are extinction."
"What does Stage 4 require?"
"Affection 75. Plus a catalyst item." She looked at him. "I'm at 58. That's 17 points in 36 hours. With combat milestones, it's achievable—if you're willing to fight beside me instead of behind me."
The question wasn't really a question. It was a challenge. A test of whether the man who'd offered saltines to a zombie was ready to become the man who'd stand with a zombie queen against an army.
Marcus pulled on his Survivor's Edge jacket—the one with the hidden knife sheath, the bite-resistant fabric, the pockets full of things that might keep him alive long enough to matter.
"Then we fight," he said.
And Sera smiled—not the predator smile, not the CEO smile, but the fierce, feral, utterly human smile of a woman who'd just been told she wasn't facing the end alone.
The clock was ticking. Thirty-five hours and forty-seven minutes.
The horde was coming.
